title: When Your Heart is On Empty
stanceSummary: A “heart like stone” can mean steadfastness under unbearable pain, not hardness before Allah; the Qur’an distinguishes a sealed heart from a heart exhausted by bearing others’ weight. Ya'qub’s swallowing of grief is paired with taking sorrow to Allah; maintaining empathy means deeds, not only tears. Captions are cleaned, not proofread.
alternateQuestions: What does it mean when someone’s heart is like a stone in Arabic culture?
How do I not become numb to suffering while fasting?
Does Allah judge me if my tears dry up?
Ya'qub swallowed his grief what does that mean?
